tool_args.label: Legislative Basis for Station Maintenance Billing

payload.claim_text: Current legislation provides that on or before June first of each year the MTA shall determine and certify to New York City and the counties the cost for station maintenance use and operations for the twelve-month period ending the preceding March thirty-first for the stations within the municipality.
payload.subject: Station maintenance billing
payload.description: Legislative requirement for MTA to determine and certify station maintenance costs annually by June 1

payload.claim_text: The MTA bills New York City and the counties each fiscal year based on a statutorily established formula adjusted annually by the CPI-W for the New York, Northeastern New Jersey and Long Island Region.
payload.subject: Station maintenance billing formula
payload.description: Billing formula uses CPI-W adjusted annually

payload.claim_text: Station Maintenance is billed in June and payment is due by September 1st.
payload.subject: Station maintenance billing timeline
payload.description: Billing occurs in June, payment due by September 1

payload.claim_text: 1998 was the last year New York City was billed on actual cost for both the LIRR and Metro North. Legislation was enacted in 1995, and renewed in 1999 and 2004, for station maintenance billing using the CPI factor and bringing NYC onboard with this method for the second time.
payload.subject: NYC station maintenance billing history
payload.description: NYC moved from actual cost billing to CPI-based formula, with legislation in 1995, renewed 1999 and 2004
